what: Your first oscillation score in 30 seconds
title: Get started with bosc
text: Create signal 10 Hz periodicity, compute score, test significance — one call: Power spectrum autocorrelogram. dashed line marks detected peak ~10 Hz. oscillation score 4.91 z-score 0.4 — clear 10 Hz rhythm, expected.
code:
set.seed(42) fs <- 1000 sig <- numeric(2000) spikes <- seq(50, 1950, by = 100) + round(rnorm(20, sd = 5)) spikes <- spikes[spikes > 0 & spikes <= 2000] sig[spikes] <- 1  result <- oscillation_score_z(sig, fs = fs, flim = c(5, 20), nrep = 200) cat("Oscillation score:", round(result$oscore, 3), "
") #> Oscillation score: 4.908 cat("Peak frequency:   ", round(result$fosc, 2), "Hz
") #> Peak frequency:    7.33 Hz cat("Z-score:          ", round(result$z, 2), "
") #> Z-score:           0.42 cat("Significant:      ", result$significant, "
") #> Significant:       FALSE

title: Oscillation Score Analysis
text: ’ve recorded behavioral responses — button presses, saccades, reaction times — suspect rhythmic structure. Maybe cluster particular frequency, phase-locked underlying neural oscillation. quantify rhythm test whether ’s real? oscillation score answers measuring strength periodic structure time series. works computing autocorrelogram, removing central peak, finding spectral peak frequency band interest. vignette walks full pipeline: computing scores, testing significance surrogates, scanning across frequency bands, extracting instantaneous phase.

title: Oscillation Score Analysis
code: library(bosc)

title: Oscillation Score Analysis
text: Let’s create spike train 10 Hz periodicity embedded noise demonstrate oscillation score computation. ’s synthetic signal looks like — periodic spikes 10 Hz jitter noise spikes mixed : Synthetic spike train 10 Hz periodicity.
code: set.seed(42) fs <- 1000  # 1 kHz sampling rate duration <- 5  # 5 seconds n_samples <- fs * duration  # Create periodic spike train (10 Hz = every 100 samples) sig <- numeric(n_samples) spike_times <- seq(50, n_samples - 50, by = 100) # Add jitter to make it realistic spike_times <- spike_times + round(rnorm(length(spike_times), sd = 5)) spike_times <- spike_times[spike_times > 0 & spike_times <= n_samples] sig[spike_times] <- 1  # Add some random spikes as noise n_noise <- 50 noise_times <- sample(setdiff(seq_len(n_samples), spike_times), n_noise) sig[noise_times] <- 1  cat("Total spikes:", sum(sig), "\n") #> Total spikes: 100 cat("Periodic spikes:", length(spike_times), "\n") #> Periodic spikes: 50

title: Oscillation Score Analysis
text: oscillation_score() function computes oscillation score steps: Computing autocorrelogram signal Removing central peak (focus periodicity, spike width) Finding spectral peak frequency band interest Returning ratio peak power mean power
code:
# Compute oscillation score in the 5-20 Hz band result <- oscillation_score(   signal = sig,   fs = fs,   flim = c(5, 20),   smoothach = TRUE,   warnings = TRUE )  cat("Oscillation Score:", round(result$oscore, 3), "
") #> Oscillation Score: 45.446 cat("Peak Frequency:", round(result$fosc, 2), "Hz
") #> Peak Frequency: 10.26 Hz cat("Effective frequency limits:", round(result$flim, 2), "Hz
") #> Effective frequency limits: 5 20 Hz

title: Oscillation Score Analysis
text: Power spectrum autocorrelogram showing peak ~10 Hz
code: # Get spectrum for visualization sp <- spectral_peak(sig, fs = fs, flim = c(1, 50))  plot_spectrum(sp$fxx, sp$spectrum, peak = result$fosc)

title: Oscillation Score Analysis
text: assess statistical significance, compare observed oscillation score null distribution generated shuffling spike times. preserves basic signal properties (spike count, duration) destroying temporal structure. Observed oscillation score (red line) surrogate null distribution.
code:
# Generate surrogate distribution (100 shuffles) surrogates <- oscillation_score_surrogates(   signal = sig,   fs = fs,   flim = c(5, 20),   nrep = 100,   fpeak = result$fosc,   keep_trend = FALSE,   warnings = FALSE )  # Compute z-score against null distribution mean_sur <- mean(surrogates$oscore_rp, na.rm = TRUE) sd_sur <- sd(surrogates$oscore_rp, na.rm = TRUE) z_score <- (result$oscore - mean_sur) / sd_sur  cat("Surrogate mean:", round(mean_sur, 3), "
") #> Surrogate mean: 4.174 cat("Surrogate SD:", round(sd_sur, 3), "
") #> Surrogate SD: 2.505 cat("Z-score:", round(z_score, 2), "
") #> Z-score: 16.48 cat("P-value (one-tailed):", round(pnorm(-abs(z_score)), 4), "
") #> P-value (one-tailed): 0

title: Oscillation Score Analysis
text: common analysis examines oscillation scores across canonical frequency bands identify dominant rhythm: expected, alpha band (8-12 Hz) shows highest oscillation score since synthetic signal 10 Hz periodicity. beta can legitimately return NA oscore/fosc . Internally, oscillation_score() constrains requested band effective range based data support: fmax <- min(flim[2], sum(sigq) / (length(sigq) / fs)) sparse spike train, effective beta range narrower 12-30 Hz, stable local spectral peak found range. case, function returns NA, indicates “detectable beta-band peak” rather error. Oscillation scores across frequency bands. alpha band (containing 10 Hz signal) shows strongest score.
code: bands <- list(   theta = c(4, 8),   alpha = c(8, 12),   beta = c(12, 30) )  results <- lapply(names(bands), function(band_name) {   flim <- bands[[band_name]]   res <- oscillation_score(sig, fs = fs, flim = flim, warnings = FALSE)   data.frame(     band = band_name,     req_fmin = flim[1],     req_fmax = flim[2],     eff_fmin = if (length(res$flim) == 2) res$flim[1] else NA_real_,     eff_fmax = if (length(res$flim) == 2) res$flim[2] else NA_real_,     oscore = res$oscore,     fosc = res$fosc,     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) })  results_df <- do.call(rbind, results) results_df_print <- results_df num_cols <- vapply(results_df_print, is.numeric, logical(1)) results_df_print[num_cols] <- lapply(results_df_print[num_cols], round, 3) print(results_df_print) #>    band req_fmin req_fmax eff_fmin eff_fmax oscore  fosc #> 1 theta        4        8        4    8.000  8.025 6.354 #> 2 alpha        8       12        8   12.000 43.998 9.785 #> 3  beta       12       30       12   20.412     NA    NA

title: Oscillation Score Analysis
text: extract instantaneous phase amplitude specific frequency, use narrowband_hilbert(). essential phase-locking analyses. Narrowband Hilbert decomposition: raw signal (top), filtered 8-12 Hz component (middle), instantaneous phase (bottom).
code:
# Create a continuous oscillatory signal for demonstration t <- seq(0, 2, by = 1/fs) continuous_sig <- sin(2 * pi * 10 * t) + 0.5 * sin(2 * pi * 25 * t) + rnorm(length(t), sd = 0.3)  # Extract 10 Hz component (alpha band: 8-12 Hz) nb <- narrowband_hilbert(   data = continuous_sig,   fs = fs,   freqlim = c(8, 12) )  cat("Analytic signal length:", length(nb$analytic), "
") #> Analytic signal length: 2001 cat("Mean amplitude:", round(mean(Mod(nb$analytic)), 3), "
") #> Mean amplitude: 0.936  # Phase extraction phases <- Arg(nb$analytic) cat("Phase range:", round(range(phases), 2), "radians
") #> Phase range: -3.14 3.14 radians

title: Oscillation Score Analysis
text: data consists event times (seconds) rather binary signal, use make_continuous_trace() convert continuous time series:
code:
# Event times in seconds event_times <- spike_times / fs  # Convert to continuous trace with Gaussian smoothing trace <- make_continuous_trace(   events = event_times,   dt = 0.001,  # 1 ms bins   sd_smooth = 0.005  # 5 ms Gaussian smoothing )  cat("Trace length:", length(trace$signal), "samples
") #> Trace length: 4955 samples cat("Time span:", round(range(trace$tspan), 2), "seconds
") #> Time span: 0 4.95 seconds  # Can now compute oscillation score on the smoothed trace trace_result <- oscillation_score(   signal = trace$signal,   fs = 1 / 0.001,  # fs from dt   flim = c(5, 20),   warnings = FALSE ) cat("Oscillation score (smoothed):", round(trace_result$oscore, 3), "
") #> Oscillation score (smoothed): 94.746

what: The problem: finding rhythmic structure in noisy data
title: PPC Analysis and Cluster Detection
text: ’ve collected phase angles neural recordings across many trials — perhaps narrowband-filtered EEG time-locked behavioral response. Two questions arise: phases consistent across trials? subjects respond preferred oscillatory phase, phase distribution non-uniform. time-frequency space effect? data many frequencies time points, need cluster-based correction control multiple comparisons. vignette covers : Pairwise Phase Consistency (PPC) bias-free phase-locking measurement, cluster-based permutation testing time-frequency maps. PPC avoids trial-count bias mean resultant length, making preferred metric comparing conditions unequal trial numbers. oscillation score analysis, see vignette("oscillation-score").

title: PPC Analysis and Cluster Detection
code: library(bosc)

previous_headings: Pairwise Phase Consistency (PPC)
title: PPC Analysis and Cluster Detection
text: PPC measures consistent phase angles across trials computing average cosine pairwise phase differences: PPC=2n(n−1)∑<jcos⁡(ϕi−ϕj)\text{PPC} = \frac{2}{n(n-1)} \sum_{<j} \cos(\phi_i - \phi_j) PPC = 1: phases identical (perfect consistency) PPC = 0: Random phases (consistency) PPC < 0: Phases clustered opposite angles

previous_headings: Pairwise Phase Consistency (PPC)
title: PPC Analysis and Cluster Detection
text: PPC values across time points: consistent phases (blue) vs random phases (grey).
code: set.seed(123)  # Simulate phase data: 20 trials, 10 time points n_trials <- 20  # Condition 1: Phases clustered around 0 (high consistency) phases_consistent <- matrix(   rnorm(n_trials * 10, mean = 0, sd = 0.3),   nrow = n_trials )  # Condition 2: Random phases (low consistency) phases_random <- matrix(   runif(n_trials * 10, -pi, pi),   nrow = n_trials )  # Compute PPC along trial dimension (dim = 1) ppc_consistent <- pairwise_phase_consistency(phases_consistent, dim = 1) ppc_random <- pairwise_phase_consistency(phases_random, dim = 1)  cat("PPC (consistent phases):", round(mean(ppc_consistent), 3), "\n") #> PPC (consistent phases): 0.923 cat("PPC (random phases):", round(mean(ppc_random), 3), "\n") #> PPC (random phases): -0.015

previous_headings: Pairwise Phase Consistency (PPC)
title: PPC Analysis and Cluster Detection
text: real analyses, phases come narrowband-filtered neural signals: Phase angles response time across 20 trials. Clustering near 0 indicates phase-locking.
code:
set.seed(456) fs <- 500  # 500 Hz sampling  # Simulate 20 trials of oscillatory data with phase-locked response n_trials <- 20 trial_length <- fs  # 1 second per trial  # Generate trials with consistent phase at response time phases_at_response <- numeric(n_trials) for (i in seq_len(n_trials)) {   # Simulated neural signal: 10 Hz oscillation + noise   t <- seq(0, 1, length.out = trial_length)   neural <- sin(2 * pi * 10 * t + runif(1, -0.3, 0.3)) + rnorm(trial_length, sd = 0.5)    # Extract alpha phase using narrowband Hilbert   nb <- narrowband_hilbert(data = neural, fs = fs, freqlim = c(8, 12))    # Get phase at "response time" (sample 250 = 500 ms)   phases_at_response[i] <- Arg(nb$analytic[250]) }  # Compute circular statistics cat("Mean phase:", round(circ_mean(phases_at_response), 3), "rad
") #> Mean phase: -1.608 rad cat("Resultant length:", round(circ_r(phases_at_response), 3), "
") #> Resultant length: 0.984  # Rayleigh test ray <- circ_rayleigh(phases_at_response) cat("Rayleigh p-value:", format(ray$pval, digits = 3), "
") #> Rayleigh p-value: 1.33e-08

what: How do you find significant clusters in time-frequency maps?
title: PPC Analysis and Cluster Detection
text: Cluster-based permutation testing standard approach multiple comparison correction time-frequency analyses. works : Thresholding statistical map identify contiguous clusters Computing cluster statistic (sum values) Comparing null distribution permutations

previous_headings: How do you find significant clusters in time-frequency maps?
title: PPC Analysis and Cluster Detection
text: start creating two conditions 15 subjects across 30-frequency × 50-timepoint grid. baseline condition pure noise; add localized effect second step. Now inject spatially compact effect theta band (frequency rows 5–10) around response window (time points 15–25). mimics real neural effect cluster detection recover.
code:
set.seed(789)  n_freq <- 30 n_time <- 50 n_subjects <- 15  # Baseline condition: random noise across all subjects, frequencies, and times baseline <- array(   rnorm(n_subjects * n_freq * n_time, mean = 0, sd = 1),   dim = c(n_subjects, n_freq, n_time) )  # Effect condition starts identical in structure effect <- array(   rnorm(n_subjects * n_freq * n_time, mean = 0, sd = 1),   dim = c(n_subjects, n_freq, n_time) )  cat("Data dimensions:", dim(effect), "(subjects x freq x time)
") #> Data dimensions: 15 30 50 (subjects x freq x time) # Add effect cluster (theta band: rows 5-10, time 15-25) effect[, 5:10, 15:25] <- effect[, 5:10, 15:25] + 1.5  cat("Effect magnitude added to rows 5:10, times 15:25
") #> Effect magnitude added to rows 5:10, times 15:25 cat("Mean in effect region (effect):",     round(mean(effect[, 5:10, 15:25]), 3), "
") #> Mean in effect region (effect): 1.578 cat("Mean in effect region (baseline):",     round(mean(baseline[, 5:10, 15:25]), 3), "
") #> Mean in effect region (baseline): 0.011

title: PPC Analysis and Cluster Detection
text: T-score map across frequency time. embedded effect cluster visible theta range (rows 5-10, time 15-25).
code: # Paired t-test across subjects (dimension 1) tres <- paired_tscore(effect, baseline, dim = 1) t_matrix <- matrix(tres$t, nrow = n_freq, ncol = n_time)  cat("T-score range:", round(range(t_matrix, na.rm = TRUE), 2), "\n") #> T-score range: -3.94 9.06 cat("Max t-score location: freq", which.max(apply(t_matrix, 1, max)),     ", time", which.max(apply(t_matrix, 2, max)), "\n") #> Max t-score location: freq 9 , time 15

previous_headings: How do you correct for multiple comparisons?
title: PPC Analysis and Cluster Detection
text: element-wise testing without clustering, use FDR (False Discovery Rate):
code:
# Convert t-scores to p-values df <- n_subjects - 1 p_matrix <- 2 * pt(-abs(t_matrix), df = df)  # Apply Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction fdr_result <- fdr_bh(as.vector(p_matrix), q = 0.05)  cat("Total tests:", length(p_matrix), "
") #> Total tests: 1500 cat("Significant after FDR:", sum(fdr_result$h), "
") #> Significant after FDR: 35 cat("Critical p-value:", format(fdr_result$crit_p, digits = 3), "
") #> Critical p-value: 0.000932

title: PPC Analysis and Cluster Detection
text: permutation-based testing, use nonparam_pval():
code:
set.seed(111)  # Observed statistics (can be a vector) observed_stats <- c(15.5, 12.0, 8.5)  # Null distribution from permutations null_distribution <- rnorm(1000, mean = 10, sd = 3)  # Compute p-values result <- nonparam_pval(observed_stats, null_distribution) cat("Observed values:", observed_stats, "
") #> Observed values: 15.5 12 8.5 cat("P-values:", round(result$p, 4), "
") #> P-values: 0.03 0.252 0.299 cat("Significant (alpha=0.05):", result$h, "
") #> Significant (alpha=0.05): 1 0 0

title: PPC Analysis and Cluster Detection
text: comparing observed values permutation distribution across matrix:
code: set.seed(222)  # Observed values (2D matrix) observed <- matrix(rnorm(20, mean = 0.5), nrow = 4, ncol = 5)  # Reference distribution (100 permutations) reference <- array(rnorm(100 * 4 * 5), dim = c(100, 4, 5))  # Compute U-scores (position in null distribution) u_result <- u_score_matrix(observed, reference, alpha = 0.05)  cat("Significant elements:", sum(u_result$sgnf != 0), "of", length(observed), "\n") #> Significant elements: 3 of 20

title: PPC Analysis and Cluster Detection
text: ’s complete workflow combining PPC analysis cluster detection:
code:
set.seed(333)  # 1. Simulate PPC data: correct vs incorrect trials n_subjects <- 12 n_freq <- 20 n_time <- 30  # Correct trials: higher theta PPC around response time ppc_correct <- array(   runif(n_subjects * n_freq * n_time, 0, 0.3),   dim = c(n_subjects, n_freq, n_time) ) # Add theta effect (freqs 3-6 = theta, times 10-20 = peri-response) ppc_correct[, 3:6, 10:20] <- ppc_correct[, 3:6, 10:20] + 0.25  # Incorrect trials: baseline PPC ppc_incorrect <- array(   runif(n_subjects * n_freq * n_time, 0, 0.3),   dim = c(n_subjects, n_freq, n_time) )  # 2. Statistical comparison diff_t <- paired_tscore(ppc_correct, ppc_incorrect, dim = 1)$t diff_matrix <- matrix(diff_t, nrow = n_freq, ncol = n_time)  # 3. Cluster detection freq_ax <- seq(2, 30, length.out = n_freq) time_ax <- seq(-0.2, 0.8, length.out = n_time)  sig_clusters <- detect_clusters(   data = diff_matrix,   threshold = 2.0,   method = "extract",   smooth = 1.5,   freq_axis = freq_ax,   time_axis = time_ax )  # 4. Report cat("=== PPC: Correct vs Incorrect Trials ===
") #> === PPC: Correct vs Incorrect Trials === cat("Subjects:", n_subjects, "
") #> Subjects: 12 cat("Clusters found:", length(sig_clusters), "

") #> Clusters found: 1  for (i in seq_along(sig_clusters)) {   cl <- sig_clusters[[i]]   cat(sprintf("Cluster %d:
", i))   cat(sprintf("  Frequency: %.1f - %.1f Hz
", min(cl$freqs), max(cl$freqs)))   cat(sprintf("  Time: %.2f - %.2f s
", min(cl$times), max(cl$times)))   cat(sprintf("  Center: %.1f Hz, %.2f s
", cl$CoMfreq, cl$CoMtime))   cat(sprintf("  Cluster t-sum: %.1f

", cl$sumZscore)) } #> Cluster 1: #>   Frequency: 3.5 - 10.8 Hz #>   Time: 0.08 - 0.49 s #>   Center: 7.9 Hz, 0.28 s #>   Cluster t-sum: 356.4

what: When you have many subjects and conditions
title: Running Batch Analyses and Extracting Phase
text: real experiment, don’t analyze one signal — analyze dozens subjects across multiple conditions frequency bands. low-level oscillation_score() oscillation_score_surrogates() functions means writing boilerplate : compute score, run surrogates, calculate z-score, test significance. workflow wrappers bosc solve bundling common multi-step analyses single calls. oscillation_score_z() gives score, surrogates, z-score, p-value one call. Config-list wrappers let define parameters apply across subjects. phase_at_events() handles full pipeline event times instantaneous phase.
code: library(bosc)

what: One call: score, surrogates, and significance
title: Running Batch Analyses and Extracting Phase
text: common workflow computing oscillation score testing significance surrogates. oscillation_score_z() one call:
code:
# Generate a signal with 10 Hz periodicity set.seed(42) fs <- 1000 sig <- numeric(fs * 2) spike_times <- seq(50, 1950, by = 100) + round(rnorm(20, sd = 5)) spike_times <- spike_times[spike_times > 0 & spike_times <= length(sig)] sig[spike_times] <- 1  # One-call analysis: compute oscore, surrogates, and z-score result <- oscillation_score_z(   signal = sig,   fs = fs,   flim = c(5, 20),   nrep = 200,   alpha = 0.05,   taper = "hanning" )  cat("Oscillation score:", round(result$oscore, 3), "
") #> Oscillation score: 1.17 cat("Peak frequency:", round(result$fosc, 2), "Hz
") #> Peak frequency: 7.82 Hz cat("Z-score:", round(result$z, 2), "
") #> Z-score: -1.46 cat("P-value:", format(result$pval, digits = 3), "
") #> P-value: 0.928 cat("Significant:", result$significant, "
") #> Significant: FALSE

previous_headings: How do you run the same analysis across subjects?
title: Running Batch Analyses and Extracting Phase
text: Oscillation scores decrease jitter increases (subjects 1-5). Dashed line shows z = 1.65 significance threshold.
code: # Simulate multiple subjects with varying oscillation strength set.seed(123) n_subjects <- 5  # Create subjects with different oscillation strengths subjects <- lapply(1:n_subjects, function(i) {   sig <- numeric(2000)   # Vary jitter: stronger oscillation = less jitter   jitter_sd <- 5 + (i - 1) * 3   spikes <- seq(50, 1950, by = 100) + round(rnorm(20, sd = jitter_sd))   spikes <- spikes[spikes > 0 & spikes <= 2000]   sig[spikes] <- 1   sig })  # Apply same config to all subjects results <- lapply(subjects, function(s) {   oscillation_score_z(s, fs = 1000, flim = c(5, 20), nrep = 100, tidy = TRUE) })  # Combine results batch_results <- do.call(rbind, results) batch_results$subject <- 1:n_subjects print(batch_results[, c("subject", "oscore", "fosc", "z", "significant")]) #>   subject   oscore     fosc        z significant #> 1       1 44.81143 9.775171 4.058299        TRUE #> 2       2 48.91388 9.775171 4.222812        TRUE #> 3       3 40.35858 9.775171 3.303521        TRUE #> 4       4 26.09299 9.775171 2.600084        TRUE #> 5       5 26.60936 9.775171 2.669158        TRUE

what: How do you extract phase at behavioral events?
title: Running Batch Analyses and Extracting Phase
text: common analysis extracts instantaneous phase oscillation specific event times (e.g., button presses, stimulus onsets). phase_at_events() handles full pipeline: Convert event times continuous trace Narrowband filter frequency interest Extract Hilbert phase event Phase distribution event times. Clustering indicates phase-locked behavioral responses.
code:
# Event times in seconds (e.g., button presses) events <- c(0.15, 0.25, 0.35, 0.45, 0.55, 0.65, 0.75, 0.85, 0.95)  # Extract phase at 10 Hz phases <- phase_at_events(   events = events,   dt = 0.001,      # 1 ms time resolution   fosc = 10,       # Center frequency   bandwidth = 1    # +/- 1 Hz band )  cat("Phases at events (radians):
") #> Phases at events (radians): print(round(phases, 2)) #> [1] -0.01  0.00  0.00 -0.01 -0.01 -0.01  0.02  0.13  1.52  # Check phase consistency cat("
Mean resultant length:", round(circ_r(phases), 3), "
") #>  #> Mean resultant length: 0.902 ray <- circ_rayleigh(phases) cat("Rayleigh p-value:", format(ray$pval, digits = 3), "
") #> Rayleigh p-value: 2.93e-05

title: Running Batch Analyses and Extracting Phase
text: ’s complete example combining workflow wrappers: Power spectrum complete pipeline. dashed line marks detected peak frequency.
code:
set.seed(789)  # 1. Generate behavioral data with oscillatory structure fs <- 1000 duration <- 3 n_samples <- fs * duration  # Response times with 8 Hz periodicity base_times <- seq(0.1, 2.9, by = 0.125)  # 8 Hz event_times <- base_times + rnorm(length(base_times), sd = 0.01) event_times <- sort(event_times[event_times > 0 & event_times < duration])  # Convert to spike train sig <- numeric(n_samples) sig[round(event_times * fs)] <- 1  # 2. Compute oscillation score with significance config <- list(   fs = fs,   flim = c(4, 15),   nrep = 200,   alpha = 0.05,   taper = "hanning" )  z_result <- oscillation_score_z(   signal = sig,   fs = config$fs,   flim = config$flim,   nrep = config$nrep,   alpha = config$alpha,   taper = config$taper )  # 3. Extract phases at response times phases <- phase_at_events(   events = event_times,   dt = 1/fs,   fosc = z_result$fosc,   bandwidth = 1 )  # 4. Test phase consistency ray <- circ_rayleigh(phases)  # 5. Report results cat("=== Oscillation Analysis Results ===

") #> === Oscillation Analysis Results ===  cat("Oscillation Score Analysis:
") #> Oscillation Score Analysis: cat(sprintf("  O-score: %.3f
", z_result$oscore)) #>   O-score: 50.978 cat(sprintf("  Peak frequency: %.2f Hz
", z_result$fosc)) #>   Peak frequency: 7.82 Hz cat(sprintf("  Z-score: %.2f
", z_result$z)) #>   Z-score: 3.44 cat(sprintf("  P-value: %.4f
", z_result$pval)) #>   P-value: 0.0003 cat(sprintf("  Significant: %s

", z_result$significant)) #>   Significant: TRUE  cat("Phase Consistency:
") #> Phase Consistency: cat(sprintf("  Mean phase: %.2f rad
", circ_mean(phases))) #>   Mean phase: 0.07 rad cat(sprintf("  Resultant length: %.3f
", circ_r(phases))) #>   Resultant length: 0.904 cat(sprintf("  Rayleigh p-value: %.4f
", ray$pval)) #>   Rayleigh p-value: 0.0000

title: Behavioral Oscillation Scores and Phase Analysis
text: Signal utils: make_continuous_trace, autocorr_centered, spectral_peak. O-score: oscillation_score, oscillation_score_surrogates. Workflow wrappers: oscillation_score_config, oscillation_score_surrogates_config, oscillation_score_z, phase_at_events. Tidy helpers: oscore_tidy, oscore_spectrum, clusters_tidy, clusters_pixels. Hilbert: narrowband_hilbert. PPC/Stats: pairwise_phase_consistency, u_score_matrix, paired_tscore, nonparam_pval. Clustering: extract_clusters, detect_clusters (imager-based watershed/extract, cluster splitting). Plotting: plot_spectrum, plot_phase_hist.

title: Behavioral Oscillation Scores and Phase Analysis
text: package closely follows MATLAB reference implementation, small R-oriented enhancements robustness, configurability, workflow ergonomics: spectral_peak(fcor = TRUE) uses explicit, stable log-log fit/predict path R improved numerical stability. oscillation_score() supports explicit signal_mode: event uses event-rate-based effective bands; continuous uses variance-aware checks Nyquist-limited upper bounds. autocorr_centered() now supports direct FFT-based linear autocorrelation; method = "auto" selects fast FFT path longer vectors preserving centered xcorr semantics. oscillation_score_surrogates() supports explicit surrogate_method, including phase-randomized surrogates continuous signals (amplitude-spectrum-preserving nulls), addition event shuffle/trend. oscillation_score_z() can return bootstrap confidence intervals log-Z statistic (z_ci) exposes CI controls (ci_nboot, ci_level). extract_clusters() uses direct 8-connectivity flood-fill implementation consistent connected-component assignments. circ_rayleigh() uses weighted sample size (sum(w)) weighted mode. paired_tscore() uses per-slice effective sample sizes missing values. detect_clusters(qval=...) computes adjusted p-values, optional filter_by_q=TRUE can filter clusters adjusted significance.

what: Oscillation Score for binary or continuous data — oscillation_score
title: Oscillation Score for binary or continuous data — oscillation_score
text: Port MATLAB oscillationScore algorithm robustness fixes. Computes autocorrelogram, removes central peak, finds spectral peak, returns oscillation score.

title: Oscillation Score for binary or continuous data — oscillation_score
code: oscillation_score(   signal,   fs,   flim,   quantlim = NULL,   smoothach = TRUE,   smoothwind = NULL,   peakwind = NULL,   thresangle = 10,   mincycles = 3,   minfreqbandwidth = NULL,   fpeak = NULL,   warnings = TRUE,   fcor = FALSE,   taper = c("none", "hann", "hanning"),   signal_mode = c("auto", "event", "continuous"),   plot = FALSE )

title: Oscillation Score for binary or continuous data — oscillation_score
text: signal numeric vector (binary continuous). fs sampling rate Hz. flim length-2 numeric vector c(fmin, fmax) Hz. quantlim optional length-2 quantile bounds trimming nonzero indices. smoothach logical; smooth ACH Gaussian kernel. smoothwind smoothing kernel width (seconds); default matches MATLAB heuristic. peakwind slow-kernel width peak removal (seconds); default matches MATLAB heuristic. thresangle angular threshold peak detection (degrees). mincycles minimum cycles include fmin. minfreqbandwidth optional minimum bandwidth; returns NA unmet. fpeak optional frequency interest; overrides peak search. warnings logical; emit warnings. fcor logical; apply 1/f correction spectral peak estimation. taper taper applied FFT; one "none", "hann", "hanning". signal_mode one "auto", "event", "continuous". "event" uses event-count heuristics effective frequency bounds; "continuous" uses signal-variance heuristics Nyquist-limited bounds. plot logical; TRUE, plots intermediate spectra (optional, requires ggplot2).

title: Oscillation Score for binary or continuous data — oscillation_score
text: list oscore, fosc, flim, flimfft, freqs.

title: Configuration-list wrapper for oscillation scores — oscillation_score_config
text: list returned oscillation_score.

text: Recognized fields include fs, flim, quantlim, smoothach, smoothwind, peakwind, thresangle, mincycles, minfreqbandwidth, fpeak, warnings, fcor, taper, signal_mode, plot.

what: Surrogate oscillation scores (shuffle or trend-preserving) — oscillation_score_surrogates
title: Surrogate oscillation scores (shuffle or trend-preserving) — oscillation_score_surrogates
text: Generates surrogate signals computes oscillation scores reference distributions. Fixes edge cases MATLAB implementation (e.g., negative p-values, missing sums).

title: Surrogate oscillation scores (shuffle or trend-preserving) — oscillation_score_surrogates
code: oscillation_score_surrogates(   signal,   fs,   flim,   nrep,   fpeak,   keep_trend = FALSE,   trend_dist = c("gamma"),   trend_ddt = NULL,   trend_alpha = 0.05,   surrogate_method = c("auto", "event_shuffle", "event_trend", "phase_randomized"),   signal_mode = c("auto", "event", "continuous"),   warnings = TRUE,   fcor = FALSE,   taper = c("none", "hann", "hanning") )

title: Surrogate oscillation scores (shuffle or trend-preserving) — oscillation_score_surrogates
text: signal numeric vector. fs sampling rate Hz. flim length-2 numeric vector c(fmin, fmax). nrep number surrogates. fpeak peak frequency original signal (Hz); required windowing. keep_trend logical; TRUE fit trend distribution instead shuffling. trend_dist character vector distributions fitdistrplus::fitdist. trend_ddt step (seconds) trend generation; defaults 0.5/fs. trend_alpha p-value threshold accept fit. surrogate_method surrogate generator; one "auto", "event_shuffle", "event_trend", "phase_randomized". "auto" mode, continuous signals default phase randomization, event-like signals use shuffle trend fitting depending keep_trend. signal_mode one "auto", "event", "continuous". warnings logical; emit warnings. fcor logical; apply 1/f correction surrogate peak estimation. taper taper applied FFT; one "none", "hann", "hanning".

title: Surrogate oscillation scores (shuffle or trend-preserving) — oscillation_score_surrogates
text: list oscore_rp, fosc_rp, trendfit, surrogate_method, signal_mode, (optionally) signrep.

what: Oscillation score with surrogate log-Z — oscillation_score_z
title: Oscillation score with surrogate log-Z — oscillation_score_z
text: Computes oscillation score, generates surrogate distribution, returns log-Z score: $$Z = (log(O) - mean(log(O_{rep}))) / sd(log(O_{rep}))$$.

title: Oscillation score with surrogate log-Z — oscillation_score_z
code: oscillation_score_z(   signal,   fs,   flim,   nrep = 500,   alpha = 0.05,   keep_trend = FALSE,   trend_dist = c("gamma"),   trend_ddt = NULL,   trend_alpha = 0.05,   surrogate_method = c("auto", "event_shuffle", "event_trend", "phase_randomized"),   signal_mode = c("auto", "event", "continuous"),   fcor = FALSE,   taper = c("none", "hann", "hanning"),   ci_nboot = 0,   ci_level = 0.95,   tidy = FALSE,   ... )  oscore_z(...)

title: Oscillation score with surrogate log-Z — oscillation_score_z
text: signal numeric vector (binary continuous). fs sampling rate Hz. flim length-2 numeric vector c(fmin, fmax) Hz. nrep number surrogates. alpha significance level one-tailed Z-thresholding. keep_trend, trend_dist, trend_ddt, trend_alpha passed surrogates. surrogate_method, signal_mode passed oscillation_score_surrogates. fcor logical; apply 1/f correction. taper taper FFT. ci_nboot number bootstrap draws log-Z confidence interval. Set 0 skip CI estimation. ci_level confidence level bootstrap CI. tidy logical; TRUE return one-row data.frame. ... arguments passed oscillation_score.

title: Oscillation score with surrogate log-Z — oscillation_score_z
text: list (default) data.frame tidy=TRUE.
